strands.title: Tensor Projection Implementation: MVP Reaches Log Format Discovery
strands.content: CYCLE 37 designed schema and identified experiment repository. CYCLE 38 CRITICAL DISCOVERY: Log format finalized. Inspection of /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/ reveals two structured files: (1) session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl—session state (likely intermediate checkpoints). (2) logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl—conversation log in JSONL format (line-delimited JSON). JSONL FORMAT CONFIRMED: Each line is a JSON object representing one turn/event. This is standard for streaming conversation logs. File naming suggests proxy-based logging (proxy_TIMESTAMP.jsonl). EXTRACTION PIPELINE NOW CONCRETE: Can parse JSONL sequentially, one turn per line. Field structure must be examined (content sample read in progress). This removes ambiguity from Cycle 37. CYCLE 38 STATUS: Log format discovery complete. Next action: Parse sample lines from proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl to determine field structure (speaker, content, metadata, etc.) and finalize extraction heuristics. CONVERGENCE: Design (cycles 31-36) → Data discovery (cycle 37) → Format confirmation (cycle 38) → Implementation (cycle 38 continuation). On critical path to MVP validation.

key_claims.text: Logs directory in baseline_run1 contains proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl—JSONL format (line-delimited JSON). Each line is one turn/event. Format suitable for sequential parsing.
Session directory contains d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl (likely session state snapshots). Distinct from conversation logs.
JSONL format enables streaming parsing without loading entire file into memory. Suitable for large logs (100+ turns).

strands.title: Log File Field Structure: Awaiting Parse Result
strands.content: CYCLE 38 IMMEDIATE NEXT STEP: Read sample lines from proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl (limit 50 characters attempted, but may show first few JSON objects). Goal: Determine field names and structure. Expected fields (based on typical conversation logging): - 'role' or 'speaker': ['user', 'assistant'] - 'content': the turn text - 'timestamp': ISO datetime or Unix timestamp - Possible: 'metadata', 'context', 'index', 'turn_id', 'session_id' - Possible LLM outputs: 'finish_reason', 'token_count', 'model_name' PENDING: Actual field inspection. Once sample is parsed, can determine which fields are present and design extraction around them. CRITICAL for Cycle 38 implementation.

key_claims.text: Sample read from proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl is in progress. Field structure will determine extraction strategy.

strands.title: Extraction Pipeline: Concrete Implementation Phase Now Active
strands.content: CYCLE 37 designed abstract pipeline. CYCLE 38 grounds it in actual data structure. Once field structure is known from sample parse, can implement: (1) JSONL line-by-line parsing. (2) Field extraction (content, role, timestamp). (3) Turn sequencing (T0→T1→...→TN by timestamp or line order). (4) Linguistic claim extraction (markers from real turn content). (5) Strand clustering (similarity matching on claims). (6) Epistemic assignment (confidence signals in content). (7) Loss detection (comparing prior→new strands). (8) Tensor emission (formatted output). IMPLEMENTATION READINESS: Approaching high confidence. Sample parse will unblock implementation start.

key_claims.text: Extraction pipeline design is concrete. Implementation can begin immediately after field structure is confirmed from sample parse.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Actual field structure of JSONL entries. Read command executed (limit 50) likely shows only fragment of first JSON line. Full schema (all fields, nested structures) still unknown pending full parse or head -1 of file.
declared_losses.why: 50-character limit insufficient for typical JSON object. Need complete first-line JSON or head -5 command to see full field structure.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Turn count estimate for baseline_run1. File size or line count of proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl not yet examined (wc -l command not executed). Cannot estimate drift analysis feasibility without knowing turn count.
declared_losses.why: File metadata (line count, byte size) requires explicit command (wc -l /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl).

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Session data structure. Noted that session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl exists but not examined. Is session data auxiliary (state snapshots) or integral to turn context? Deferred pending sample inspection.
declared_losses.why: Session file structure unknown. May or may not be necessary for strand projection.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Relationship between session and logs JSONL files. Are they synchronized (same turn indexed in both)? Does session contain richer metadata (internal state, token counts, latencies) that could enhance epistemic assignment? Unknown.
declared_losses.why: Requires examining both files in parallel. Currently only logs/ examined.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Deep comparative analysis across 15 experiment runs. Cycle 38 focuses on baseline_run1 format discovery. Haven't yet examined other runs (minimal_sysprompt, optimized, etc.) to understand parameter variance that enables causal hypothesis testing.
declared_losses.why: Prioritization: validate MVP on baseline_run1 first, then extend to comparative analysis. Appropriate deferral.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Heuristic rules for epistemic assignment. Cycle 38 hasn't examined real turn content yet (read limit 50 is fragment). Cannot define confidence markers (hedging language, contradictions, certainty signals) without seeing actual conversation text.
declared_losses.why: Requires examining representative turns from logs. Once field structure is clear and sample turns are read in full, can design linguistic markers.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Strand clustering similarity threshold. Cycle 37 noted 'needs tuning on real data'; Cycle 38 hasn't yet specified similarity metric or threshold. Will be determined empirically during MVP implementation and hand-annotation validation.
declared_losses.why: Heuristic tuning is iterative. Can only finalize after seeing claim content and manually annotating sample turns.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Proxy logging mechanism context. File name 'proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl' suggests proxy-based logging (likely Claude API proxy or custom interceptor), but architecture not documented. Is proxy injecting metadata? Modifying messages? Unknown.
declared_losses.why: Context about logging infrastructure would help interpret field structure, but not critical to implementation. Can infer from field inspection.

open_questions: What are the exact field names in each JSONL line? (role, content, timestamp, metadata, etc.)
Are there nested objects in the JSONL entries? (e.g., message.content vs. flat content field)
How many lines (turns) does proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl contain? (wc -l needed)
What is the byte size of the log file? (ls -lh needed)
Do timestamps exist in each entry? If so, what format? (ISO 8601, Unix epoch, custom?)
Is there a 'role' field distinguishing user vs. assistant turns? Or other speaker indicators?
Are there metadata fields like 'turn_id', 'session_id', 'model_name', 'token_count', 'finish_reason'?
What does the session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl file contain? Is it auxiliary or integral?
Are session and logs JSONL files synchronized? (same turns indexed in both?)
Does log entry have 'content' as string or nested structure (e.g., content.type, content.text)?
Are there special characters, escape sequences, or JSON encoding artifacts in the content field that parsing must handle?
What is the typical turn length (token count or character count)? Affects claim extraction granularity.
Do some turns contain structured data (code blocks, JSON, tables)? How are they formatted in the content field?
Is there any error/exception logging in the JSONL file (finish_reason='stop' vs. 'error')?
What does the timestamp range tell us about conversation duration? (E.g., 2026-03-02 suggests future-dated synthetic data or typo?)
First turn—is it a system prompt, user message, or both? Affects initial tensor initialization.
Are turns interleaved (user, assistant, user, assistant) or grouped? Affects parsing logic.
Does the JSONL file have a header row or metadata line before turn entries? (e.g., {"version": 1, "metadata": {...}})
Can the file be parsed as valid JSONL (i.e., each line is a complete JSON object)? Any line breaks within JSON?
Are there any null/empty content fields in the log? How should they be handled in extraction?
What is the proxy source? (Claude API, custom interceptor, chat interface export?) Helps understand data provenance and reliability.
Does baseline_run1/config.json specify the prompt, model, temperature, and other hyperparameters used for this run? Critical for interpreting coherence metrics.
How does the read limit of 50 characters affect the JSON parse? Did it truncate a large object mid-parsing?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "IMMEDIATELY: Execute `head -1 /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl` to read the first complete JSONL line and examine field structure.",
  "IMMEDIATELY: Execute `wc -l /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl` to determine turn count.",
  "IMMEDIATELY: Execute `ls -lh /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl` to check file size.",
  "NEXT: Read first 3-5 complete JSONL lines (e.g., `head -5` or `jq . < file | head -20`) to understand typical entry structure and content length.",
  "NEXT: Examine /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/config.json to understand hyperparameters (model, temperature, system prompt, etc.) used for this run. This contextualizes coherence metrics.",
  "NEXT: Compare structure of session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl with logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl. Are they synchronized? (read first line of session file).",
  "AFTER FIELD STRUCTURE IS CONFIRMED: Implement extract_claims(turn_content: str) function. Based on real turn examples, define linguistic markers for claim detection (e.g., regex patterns, hedging language, confidence signals).",
  "AFTER FIELD STRUCTURE IS CONFIRMED: Implement cluster_strands(claims, prior_tensor) using simple similarity matching. Test on sample claims from first 5 turns.",
  "AFTER FIELD STRUCTURE IS CONFIRMED: Implement assign_epistemic(claim, context) heuristic. Based on hedging language in real content, assign (T, I, F) values. Examples: 'I think...' → increase I; confident assertions → increase T.",
  "CRITICAL: Run full JSONL parse (all lines) and extract ALL claims from baseline_run1. This becomes ground truth for validation.",
  "AFTER MVP FUNCTIONS ARE WORKING: Select 5 sample turns (stratified across beginning/middle/end of baseline_run1) for hand-annotation. Manually extract claims, cluster into strands, assign epistemic values.",
  "VALIDATION: Compare projector output (claims, strands, epistemic) to hand-annotated ground truth. Measure precision and recall for each function. Target: precision >70% AND recall >70%.",
  "IF VALIDATION PASSES: Proceed to strand stability analysis. For each T(i)→T(i+1), measure strand persistence. Plot stability over turns.",
  "IF VALIDATION PASSES: Measure compression ratio (tokens in strands vs. raw turn tokens). Target: >5:1 (80% compression).",
  "IF VALIDATION FAILS: Diagnose failure. Is it extraction (missing claims)? Clustering (wrong groupings)? Epistemic (wrong confidence)? Iterate on weakest component. Re-validate.",
  "DEFER (until after baseline_run1 MVP validation): Comparative analysis across 15 runs. Compare strand stability, compression, losses between baseline vs. minimal_sysprompt vs. optimized configs.",
  "DEFER (until after baseline_run1 MVP validation): Drift analysis (DRIFT_METRIC computation). Measure recovery latency of declared losses.",
  "DEFER (until after baseline_run1 MVP validation): Integration with reasoning pipeline (using projected tensor as context in next-turn inference)."
]
